quickFacts: Free water from nasoni (public fountains) all over the city
The Colosseum + Forum + Palatine combo ticket lasts 2 days
Coffee standing at the bar costs half the price of sitting down
Never eat at restaurants with picture menus near major sights
foodScene: Cacio e pepe (pecorino & black pepper pasta) is simple perfection. Supplì (fried rice balls) from street vendors. Trapizzino for pizza-pocket hybrids. Trastevere has great trattorias but go a block off the main drag. Gelato from Fatamorgana — no artificial colours.

places.name: Colosseum
places.caption: Gladiator arena
places.description: Book the underground and upper tier tour for areas most visitors never see. Best photos at golden hour from the viewpoint on Via Nicola Salvi.

places.name: Roman Forum & Palatine Hill
places.caption: Ancient city centre
places.description: Where Julius Caesar walked. The Forum is included with your Colosseum ticket. Palatine Hill has gardens and the best Forum views.

places.name: Trastevere
places.caption: Bohemian neighbourhood
places.description: Ivy-covered buildings, cobblestone alleys, and the heart of Roman nightlife. Cross the river and get happily lost. Best at sunset.

places.name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
places.caption: Michelangelo's ceiling
places.description: Book skip-the-line tickets or go on the last Sunday of the month (free but queued). The Raphael Rooms are just as impressive.

places.name: Pantheon
places.caption: 2,000-year-old dome
places.description: The oculus lets rain fall inside and the geometry is perfect. Free entry (reservation required). Visit when it rains for a magical effect.

places.name: Trevi Fountain
places.caption: Baroque masterpiece
places.description: Throw a coin with your right hand over your left shoulder. Come at 7am or 11pm to actually see it without being crushed by crowds.

places.name: Aventine Hill & Keyhole
places.caption: Secret viewpoint
places.description: Peek through the Knights of Malta keyhole for a perfectly framed St. Peter's dome. The Orange Garden next door has panoramic sunset views.

places.name: Testaccio
places.caption: Working-class foodie district
places.description: The real Roman food neighbourhood. Testaccio Market for street food, and the surrounding trattorias serve the best offal-based Roman classics.

places.name: Villa Borghese
places.caption: Park & world-class gallery
places.description: Rent a rowboat on the lake, then see Bernini's sculptures in the Galleria Borghese (book ahead — limited entries). A green oasis above Piazza del Popolo.

places.name: Appian Way
places.caption: Ancient Roman road
places.description: Rent a bike on Sundays (car-free) and ride past catacombs, aqueducts, and 2,300-year-old paving stones. The most atmospheric experience in Rome.

places.name: Borghese Gallery
places.caption: Bernini & Caravaggio
places.description: Exquisite collection in a villa surrounded by Rome's most beautiful park. Only 360 visitors every 2 hours — book ahead.

places.name: Piazza Navona
places.caption: Baroque square
places.description: Three fountains (Bernini's Four Rivers), street artists and gelateries on the site of a Roman stadium. Most beautiful square in Rome.
